name: A167 DURHAM WESTERN BYPASS 1/15
units: metres below ground level, transcribed verbatim from a typed site-investigation log (Wimpey Geotech, cable percussion)

intervals.verbatim: Stiff mottled orange brown and grey sandy silty CLAY with occasional fine to coarse angular gravel occasional cobble and occasional gravel of coal
annotations: coal present only as scattered gravel clasts, not a seam

intervals.verbatim: Stiff brown with occasional grey mottles slightly sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse angular to subrounded gravel
Stiff laminated brown silty CLAY with occasional fine to coarse angular and subangular gravel occasional gravel of coal some shear surfaces and possible slickensides
annotations: hand mark in structural (H) column reads 'S/S' (shear surface / slickenside), consistent with the logged shear surfaces

intervals.verbatim: Medium dense brown clayey silty fine and medium SAND with occasional fine angular gravel of coal
annotations: spans sheet 1 to sheet 2 ('See previous sheet')
unconsolidated glacial SAND, not sandstone bedrock

intervals.verbatim: Soft to firm faintly laminated orange brown clayey sandy SILT with occasional reddish brown silty clay laminations, occasional orange brown silty fine sand lamination and pocket and occasional gravel of coal
annotations: unconsolidated SILT (a soil), not siltstone bedrock

intervals.verbatim: Medium dense and dense orange brown silty fine and medium SAND with occasional brown silty clay lamination and pocket and occasional gravel of coal
annotations: spans sheet 2 to sheet 3 ('See previous sheet')
date marker 23/6 at 15.00m; water column reads DRY at 15.00m
unconsolidated glacial SAND, not sandstone bedrock

intervals.verbatim: Dense brown silty fine and medium SAND with some laminations of brown silty clay
annotations: date marker 24/6 at 22.00m; water column reads DRY at 22.00m

intervals.verbatim: Firm to stiff laminated brown very silty CLAY with occasional orange brown slightly clayey sandy silt pockets
Stiff brown slightly sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse angular to subrounded gravel
annotations: the log carries two adjacent intervals (bases 24.20m and 24.65m) with word-for-word identical descriptions; transcribed as written (see interval 11)

intervals.verbatim: Stiff brown slightly sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse angular to subrounded gravel
annotations: duplicate of interval 10's description at a second base (24.65m); not silently merged

intervals.verbatim: Firm and stiff laminated brown silty CLAY with some silt partings and occasional fine angular gravel:firm to stiff below 27.30m

intervals.verbatim: Orange brown silty CLAY with some fine to coarse angular gravel and occasional cobble
Stiff to very stiff brown very sandy silty CLAY with occasional fine and medium subrounded gravel of coal and pockets of silty fine and medium sand
Brown sandy silty CLAY with occasional fine to coarse angular to subrounded gravel:becoming silty fine and medium sand in places
Very stiff sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse angular to subrounded gravel and occasional gravel of coal
annotations: END OF BOREHOLE at 30.00m; water column reads DRY at 30.00m

notes: Modern metric site-investigation log, Wimpey Geotech (Lab Ref No S/26671), cable percussion with Pilcon Wayfarer, borehole 1/15, commenced 19/6/92, logged 30/6/92 (originator DAN, checked LKR). Ground level 92.15 mOD; local site grid E 5880.2 N 6005.1. Boring/casing diameter stepped 200mm to 22.00m then 150mm to 30.00/29.50m. Log is 3 sheets (pages 1-3); all 16 intervals reconciled against the printed OD-Level column with zero discrepancy (92.15 minus base depth matches every printed value). The entire hole is Quaternary DRIFT (glacial/glaciolacustrine stiff clays, laminated silts and medium-dense sands) to 30.00 m END OF BOREHOLE at the index depth; no bedrock, no coal seams reached. 'Gravel of coal' appears throughout as scattered clasts within the drift, NOT as any worked seam, so seam_marks is empty. Two adjacent intervals (10 and 11, bases 24.20 m and 24.65 m) carry word-for-word identical descriptions in the original; both transcribed rather than merged. Structural (H) column carries two hand 'S/S' marks (shear surface/slickenside) near 3.85-4.45 m and 25.65-26.00 m, echoing the logged shear surfaces; these are structural annotations, not pencil seam correlations. Water column reads DRY at 3.00-3.50 m, 15.00 m, 22.00 m and 30.00 m; Water Added 3.50-30.00 m and chiselling 27.75-28.00 m (0.50 h) per the remarks strip; a lockable protective steel cover was concreted over the piezometer installation. Pages 4-7 are location/site plans (Site Plan sheets, Roughton & Partners / Wimpey, 'A167 Durham Western Bypass') with no strata; pages 5-7 are heavily toned photocopy scans, illegible for content but confirmed to contain no borehole log. Interval numbering runs continuously across sheets 1-3.
